methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
AST SpaceMobile operates in the Space & Aerospace sector as a publicly traded company, having raised over $2B to date. The valuation landscape comprises 13 comparable entities: 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private competitors including Planet Labs, Astroscale, SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Impulse Space.

Standard valuation triangulation methods yield limited applicability here. Peer-median analysis cannot be executed; while 11 same-sector peers exist, the filtered set of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables falls below the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring, the secondary approach, also proves inapplicable because AST SpaceMobile's revenue—essential for price-to-sales multiples—is not on file. The Space & Aerospace public peer set trades at a 25.8x median P/S, but without the target company's disclosed revenue figure, this anchor cannot be deployed.

The absence of sufficient narrow peer data and missing revenue disclosure constrains quantitative synthesis. No triangulated valuation range can be supported by the available data. Key uncertainties include AST SpaceMobile's current revenue run-rate, margin profile relative to the public comp universe, and capital intensity relative to peers. Future valuation work would require either: (1) disclosed revenue metrics from the company or (2) expansion of the comparable set to include secondary transaction multiples from private financings.
